pattern: Tracking each value's current position in a map turns repeated replacements into constant-time edits instead of full scans.
hints: Because all values are distinct, you can remember where each value currently lives in the array.
Keep a map from value to index; an operation rewrites one slot and moves the bookkeeping from the old value to the new one.
Build a value-to-index map of the starting array. For each operation [from, to], find from's index, write to into that slot, and update the map so to now points there.

edge_inputs.description: three replacements including reuse of a freed value
chained replacements where a new value is later replaced again
replace the same slot twice via a chain
operations target different slots in non-array order
no-op style chain returning to a fresh value

stress.description: large distinct array with a chain of valid replacements
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 3000))
    pool = list(range(1, 1000001))
    rng.shuffle(pool)
    nums = pool[:n]
    present = set(nums)
    nxt = n
    ops = []
    for _ in range(n):
        frm = nums[rng.randrange(n)]
        while nxt < len(pool) and pool[nxt] in present:
            nxt += 1
        if nxt >= len(pool):
            break
        to = pool[nxt]
        nxt += 1
        present.discard(frm)
        present.add(to)
        idx = nums.index(frm)
        nums[idx] = to
        ops.append([frm, to])
    return [pool[:n], ops]
